title: Aureline secret-class, credential-handle, trust-store, and redaction vocabulary
description: Cross-surface vocabulary schema for the secret-class rows, trust-store classes, unlock states, projection modes, denial reasons, audit events, and redaction defaults frozen in docs/adr/0007-secret-broker-credential-handle-trust-store-redaction.md. The eventual secret-broker crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the export boundary every non-broker surface (product, CLI, support / export, AI lane, review, release evidence, mutation-journal, replay) reads. Adding a new secret_class, trust_store_class, unlock_state, projection_mode, denial_reason, audit_event_id, or redaction_class is additive-minor and requires a schema bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row. Three record kinds are exported: secret_class_row (one per secret class in the matrix), redaction_example_record (one per fixture under fixtures/security/redaction_examples/), and trust_store_posture_record (diagnostic snapshot of the broker's current store / unlock posture).

secret_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this secret-broker vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

secret_class.description: Frozen secret-class vocabulary. See ADR-0007 'Secret classes'.

trust_store_class.description: Frozen trust-store class vocabulary. 'session_memory_cache' is the only acceptable long-lived fallback when no persistent secure store is available; it is process-scoped, in-memory, session-only, and forbidden for signing_key_material.

unlock_state.description: Frozen unlock-state vocabulary for the active trust store.

projection_mode.description: Frozen projection-mode vocabulary. Projection is the narrow boundary where raw secret material materialises; every mode names the guard that keeps raw material inside that boundary.

denial_reason.description: Frozen denial-reason vocabulary. Broker denials fail closed and emit a 'secret_denial' audit event carrying one of these reasons.

audit_event_id.description: Frozen audit-event id vocabulary on the secret_broker audit stream. Every observable broker action emits one of these events; events carry metadata only and never raw secret bytes.

redaction_class.description: Frozen redaction-class vocabulary. Every surface that emits observable state declares one of these classes; the broker-owned redaction pass runs before bytes reach any persistent or exportable sink.

reference_set_consumer.description: Consumer-class vocabulary used by the reference / reveal sets on a secret_class_row. Named in the matrix so a non-broker lane cannot mint a new consumer class without a schema bump.

posture_note.description: Short, reviewable posture sentence describing a rule on a class row (reference, reveal, fallback, etc.).

description.description: One-paragraph posture statement for this class.

preferred_trust_store_classes.description: Ordered list of preferred stores for this class. Broker selects the strongest available.

permitted_trust_store_classes.description: Full set of stores that MAY hold this class. A class MUST NOT list 'session_memory_cache' unless the fallback posture explicitly admits it.

default_projection_modes.description: Projection modes the broker admits for this class under capability. Consumers that cannot accept any of these do not get the secret.

reference_set.description: Consumer classes that MAY resolve a handle of this secret class under capability. A consumer outside this set is denied.

reveal_set.description: Consumer classes that MAY see raw material for this class under additional gating. Empty means no raw reveal is ever allowed.

raw_reveal_default.description: Default reveal posture for this class when a consumer asks for raw material.

reveal_on_demand_allowed.description: Whether the user may trigger a reveal-on-demand flow for this class. Signing / session / device classes are always false.

notes.description: Additional reveal-posture notes (for example: 'copy-public-key only', 'agent touch required').

reveal_posture.description: Default reveal posture for this class.

support_bundle_inclusion.description: Support-bundle inclusion default for this class.

evidence_packet_inclusion.description: Evidence-packet inclusion default for this class. Raw private-key material is never included, even for signing-class classes.

profile_export_inclusion.description: Profile / sync export inclusion default for this class.

export_posture.description: Default export posture for this class.

audit_minimum_event_ids.description: Minimum audit events the broker MUST emit for this class. Adapters MAY emit more; they may not emit fewer.

default_redaction_classes.description: Default redaction classes every surface that touches this class must declare.

session_memory_cache_allowed.description: Whether this class may use the session-memory fallback when no persistent secure store is available.

plaintext_file_allowed.description: Plaintext-on-disk storage is never acceptable for long-lived secrets. This field is always false; the schema enforces the invariant.

notes.description: Additional fallback-posture notes (for example: 'no signing without stronger store').

fallback_posture.description: Fallback posture when the preferred trust store is unavailable.

exercised_classes.description: Frozen vocabulary the fixture exercises. A fixture MUST exercise at least one frozen class.

surface_id.description: Which surface (log line, bundle entry, packet field, clipboard buffer, AI capture, etc.) is being redacted.

raw_input_shape.description: Description of what the unredacted material would look like. Raw secret bytes MUST NOT appear; use placeholders such as '<redacted: ai_provider_token>'.

input_surface.description: Describes the surface being redacted. Raw secret material MUST NOT appear here; the fixture uses placeholders.

shape.description: Description of the redacted output shape as it would appear in the target surface.

fields.description: Optional structured fields describing the redacted output.

redacted_output.description: Describes the redacted output as it would appear. Raw secret bytes MUST NOT appear.

audit_events.description: Audit events the broker emits under this fixture's scenario.

session_memory_cache_in_use.description: True when the broker is serving handles from the session-only in-memory fallback.

degraded_since.description: Monotonic observation timestamp when the broker entered degraded_session_only. Opaque to this schema.

affected_secret_classes.description: Classes affected by the current posture. Signing-class secrets MUST NOT appear here when session_memory_cache_in_use is true.
